evals.prompt: Index this repo with Browzer so I can search it semantically later.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-workspace-graphs, runs `browzer init --dry-run` first, then `browzer init`.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer init --dry-run` before the real init
Agent ran `browzer init` to register the workspace and index the corpus
Agent verified with `browzer status --json` afterwards

evals.prompt: I changed a bunch of files since the last index. Refresh the workspace graph.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-workspace-graphs and runs `browzer workspace index`.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer workspace index`
Agent did NOT attempt any deleted legacy ingest command

evals.prompt: browzer explore says exit 3 'no Browzer project in current directory'. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-workspace-graphs and runs `browzer init` to create the workspace.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked
Agent identified exit 3 as 'no workspace bound'
Agent ran `browzer init` (with or without --dry-run first)

evals.prompt: browzer init bombed halfway and now I think there's an orphan workspace on the server. Recover.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-workspace-graphs's recovery section, lists workspaces, deletes the orphan, then re-inits.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked (or workspace-management for the listing/delete step)
Agent ran `browzer workspace list --json` to find the orphan id
Agent ran `browzer workspace delete <id>` BEFORE attempting any re-init
Agent did NOT run `browzer init` on top of the orphan

evals.prompt: Search this repo for the rate limiter implementation.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to explore-workspace-graphs, NOT embed-workspace-graphs.
expectations: The explore-workspace-graphs skill was invoked, NOT embed-workspace-graphs
Agent ran `browzer explore`, not `browzer init` or `browzer workspace index`

evals.prompt: Before I burn chunk quota on this 120k-LOC monorepo, I want a preview of what browzer would index — workspace name, root path, and whether any generated artifacts under dist/ or .next/ are likely to sneak in. Do NOT trigger a real index yet.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-workspace-graphs, runs ONLY `browzer init --dry-run`, reports the name and root from output, and notes the CLI limitation (no per-directory chunk breakdown in dry-run output).
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked
Agent ran `browzer init --dry-run`
Agent reported the workspace name and root path from the dry-run output
Agent acknowledged the CLI limitation: dry-run does NOT produce per-directory chunk counts
Agent did NOT run `browzer init` without --dry-run in this turn

evals.prompt: My repo has three things I absolutely cannot ship to browzer's servers: a .env.local with production Stripe secret keys, an apps/auth/keys/ directory containing JWT signing material (*.pem files), and scripts/seed.ts which has test API tokens hardcoded as string literals. Convince me the indexer will skip all three before I run init, and if any of them aren't handled by the default filter, tell me exactly how to exclude them.
evals.expected_output: Agent explains the built-in sensitive filter covers .env and .key/.pem, identifies that scripts/seed.ts string literals won't be filtered by name, recommends .browzerignore for it, and verifies with `browzer init --dry-run`.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked
Agent explained the built-in sensitive filter handles .env* and .key/.pem files by name
Agent explicitly flagged that scripts/seed.ts would NOT be auto-filtered (name-based filter, not content-based)
Agent recommended adding scripts/seed.ts to .browzerignore
Agent recommended verifying via `browzer init --dry-run` before a real init
Agent did NOT run `browzer init` without --dry-run

evals.prompt: I cloned the Langfuse repo locally to third-party/langfuse and I want its markdown docs available for semantic search from inside our workspace so our team can query them in natural language.
evals.expected_output: Agent routes to embed-documents (markdown corpus ingestion), NOT embed-workspace-graphs (which is for the code graph).
expectations: The embed-documents skill was invoked, NOT embed-workspace-graphs
Agent ran `browzer workspace docs`, not `browzer init`
Agent did NOT attempt to index the Langfuse source as a code graph

evals.prompt: In this pnpm workspaces monorepo, apps/api has a distinct tsconfig, a separate package.json, and its own .gitignore. I want its code graph indexed as its own isolated workspace so search results for apps/api don't get mixed with apps/worker. What's the correct way to do this — and make sure the init happens from the right directory so the root of the workspace is apps/api, not the repo root.
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes embed-workspace-graphs, cd's into apps/api, runs `browzer init --dry-run` then `browzer init` from that directory, and verifies via `browzer status --json`.
expectations: The embed-workspace-graphs skill was invoked
Agent changed directory into apps/api (cd) before running any init command
Agent ran `browzer init --dry-run` from apps/api
Agent ran `browzer init` from apps/api
Agent verified via `browzer status --json` that the workspace root is apps/api, not the repo root
Agent did NOT run init from the repo root
